title: firecrawl
summary: Firecrawl is a TypeScript monorepo web-data API (106K stars, AGPL-3.0, 13 sub-apps including 7 polyglot SDKs) organized around a pluggable scraping engine with fallback-list architecture (scrapeURL -> buildFallbackList -> scrapeURLWithEngine -> NoEnginesLeftError). Core value: the Search/Scrape/Crawl/Map/Interact/Agent taxonomy plus a 58-app examples cookbook pairing firecrawl with every major LLM. Key transferable patterns for SoNash: engine-fallback chain for website-analysis, capability-gated test pattern from CLAUDE.md, multi-agent openai_swarm reference. Documentation debt is the main cost (zero architecture prose docs, one mermaid diagram). Composite Healthy (75.5): test infra 85, reliability 78, architecture 75, docs 72, security 70, maintainability 68.
creator_view: Firecrawl understands that web extraction for AI is fundamentally different from traditional scraping. Three novel paradigms: (1) Map — discover all URLs on a site instantly (URL routing/discovery), (2) Interact — scrape then interact with page via AI prompts before extracting (agent-like extraction), (3) Agent — 'describe what you need' fully autonomous data gathering. Also: batch scrape async, search+scrape combined, MCP integration. TypeScript, 105K stars, AGPL-3.0.

candidates.name: scrapeURL engine-fallback chain
candidates.description: Pluggable extraction engines coordinated by a fallback-list builder. buildFallbackList → scrapeURLWithEngine → (success?) → try next or NoEnginesLeftError. Named failure mode, documented order, engine-level telemetry.

candidates.name: Capability-gated test pattern
candidates.description: Gate tests by runtime capability (env vars) not by CI fence. Same test file runs in all environments; gate decides whether assertions execute. Three lines per gate.

candidates.name: Harness-as-boot-orchestrator
candidates.description: Single harness.ts script owns service boot order (API + workers + emulator) before test runner. CLAUDE.md mandates it; no ad-hoc `pnpm start` in dev docs.

candidates.name: Version-parallel controller trees
candidates.description: Parallel v0/v1/v2 controller directories with their own test fixtures. New features land on v2; v0 stays alive for compat. Three OpenAPI specs frozen at repo root.

candidates.name: Redlock distributed cache serialization
candidates.description: Use redlock.using([key], ttlMs, ...) to serialise cache updates across multiple pods. Prevents race conditions on shared cache slots (e.g. ACUC credit updates).

candidates.name: Multi-agent orchestration via openai_swarm
candidates.description: Reference implementation of multi-agent coordination around a single scraping task. Worth reading to inform SoNash agent teams (.claude/teams/) and the agent boundary design work for JASON-OS.

candidates.name: Deep Research API usage pattern
candidates.description: How firecrawl's Deep Research API is invoked in practice (apartment-finder example). Interactive preference gathering → research call → LLM analysis → structured JSON. Compare to SoNash /deep-research skill's searcher pipeline.

candidates.name: GitHub repo analysis via firecrawl Map API
candidates.description: examples/gemini-github-analyzer uses firecrawl's Map API to walk a GitHub repo. Direct domain overlap with SoNash's /repo-analysis — worth comparing extraction approaches.

candidates.name: Docs-to-OpenAPI extraction
candidates.description: examples/turning_docs_into_api_specs — convert a documentation site into an OpenAPI spec via scraping. Informs SoNash document-analysis handler — a docs site IS a kind of structured document.

candidates.name: Commercial-OSS feature tiering via .env
candidates.description: Fire-engine is hosted-only; self-hosted users get documented limited CAPTCHA handling. Pattern: tier features by backing service availability, document the split explicitly.

candidates.name: scrapeURL engine-fallback README
candidates.description: Mermaid signal flow diagram for the engine fallback chain. The whole extraction thesis in four nodes. Worth re-reading when expanding SoNash website-analysis.

candidates.name: 58-example cookbook pattern
candidates.description: The examples/ directory as product-marketing-in-code. 58 self-contained apps, each pairing firecrawl with a specific LLM or framework. Stealable pattern for SoNash skill documentation.

candidates.name: openai_swarm_firecrawl example
candidates.description: Concrete multi-agent coordination code. Direct reference for SoNash's agent team boundary design.

candidates.name: gemini-github-analyzer example
candidates.description: Repo-walking via firecrawl Map API, same domain as SoNash repo-analysis.

candidates.name: deep-research-apartment-finder example
candidates.description: Deep Research API usage pattern from a Python CLI.

candidates.name: turning_docs_into_api_specs example
candidates.description: Docs-to-OpenAPI extraction pattern. Informs SoNash document-analysis.

candidates.name: CLAUDE.md minimal-actionable exemplar
candidates.description: 19-line CLAUDE.md that beats most 200-line variants. Test-first, E2E preferred, capability-gating, harness usage. Worth a direct comparison against SoNash's 235-line CLAUDE.md for signal density.

candidates.name: Zero architecture prose docs
candidates.description: 1162 files, one mermaid diagram, no docs/ directory. Queue layout, auth flow, service boundaries all have to be inferred from code. New contributors pay the ramp-up tax. SoNash should file this as evidence in the next 'do we need this ADR?' conversation.

candidates.name: Two queue systems coexisting without migration plan
candidates.description: BullMQ (Redis) and nuq-postgres (custom Postgres queue) both active in services/. Rationale undocumented. Suggests in-progress migration with no exit criteria. If SoNash ever needs a queue, pick one and commit — don't run two.

candidates.name: Hand-rolled polyglot SDK parity
candidates.description: Seven SDKs (JS, Python, Rust, Go, Java, Elixir, playwright-service) re-implement the wire contract manually. Parity enforced by OpenAPI specs + test-suite app. Visible tax: adding an endpoint is a 7-file change. Correct answer for SoNash if ever pressured: generate from OpenAPI or say no.

candidates.name: Hidden feature flags via env vars
candidates.description: Firecrawl branches behaviour through SELF_HOST.md env vars (USE_DB_AUTHENTICATION, PROXY_SERVER, SEARXNG_*) with no documented feature-flag layer. Fine at small-team scale, breaks down as team grows. Prefer explicit feature flags over env-var branching for user-visible behaviour.

creatorLens: Firecrawl understands that web extraction for AI is fundamentally different from traditional scraping. Three novel paradigms: (1) Map — discover all URLs on a site instantly (URL routing/discovery), (2) Interact — scrape then interact with page via AI prompts before extracting (agent-like extraction), (3) Agent — 'describe what you need' fully autonomous data gathering. Also: batch scrape async, search+scrape combined, MCP integration. TypeScript, 105K stars, AGPL-3.0.

context.t28Relevance: Fills Gap Agent A Q2 (URL routing/discovery). Novel 'Interact' and 'Agent' paradigms blur extraction/analysis boundary. TypeScript. But AGPL-3.0.
notablePatterns: Map endpoint — URL discovery/classification for entire sites
Interact — scrape then AI-interact before extraction
